data.title: SGX Opens Higher As STI Climbs 0.36% In Cautious Global Start
data.description:
Singapore equities opened firmer, tracking a broadly cautious but positive tone across global markets as investors weighed corporate earnings momentum, central bank policy signals and ongoing geopolitical risks.

The Straits Times Index (STI) rose 17.77 points or 0.36% to 4,910.50 as of 9:07am, supported by early buying interest in selected index counters. Market breadth was positive with 136 gainers against 70 decliners.

Trading activity remained moderate in early session flow, with total volume at 187.37 million shares worth S$188.34 million.

Regional sentiment was shaped by a mixed Wall Street finish overnight, where US equities posted modest gains. The S&P 500 added 0.12% while the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.20%, both hovering near record levels, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.13%.

Investors in the US remained cautious amid a packed week featuring major corporate earnings from technology giants including Amazon, Alphabet, Meta Platforms, Apple and Microsoft. Market participants are closely watching whether heavy artificial intelligence investments are translating into stronger returns.

Sentiment has also been supported by a strong earnings season so far, with 81% of S&P 500 companies beating estimates and analysts lifting profit growth forecasts to 16.1% year on year.

However, gains remain tempered by geopolitical uncertainty and monetary policy focus, with the US Federal Reserve expected to hold interest rates steady at its upcoming meeting while signalling its outlook on inflation and economic conditions.

Back in Singapore, derivatives trading saw modest activity with 18.01K contracts. MSCI Singapore Index Futures for May 2026 traded at 440.50, while FTSE China A50 Index Futures for April 2026 were last at 15,526.00.

Regional futures were also steady, with Nikkei 225 Index Futures for June 2026 at 60,405.00 and SGX FTSE Taiwan Index Futures for April 2026 at 3,263.25.

data.title: Late Selling Pushes Bursa Into The Red
data.description:
A wave of last-minute selling erased Bursa Malaysia’s earlier gains on April 27, dragging the benchmark index into negative territory as investors rotated into mid- and small-cap counters with stronger domestic themes and clearer earnings prospects.

The FTSE Bursa Malaysia KLCI (FBM KLCI) slipped 3.07 points, or 0.17%, to close at its intraday low of 1,717.27, reversing from a session high of 1,731.79.

The late pullback ended a day of positive trading momentum, underscoring a market increasingly driven by sector rotation rather than broad-based buying.

While the blue-chip benchmark weakened, broader market segments outperformed.

The FBM 70 rose 96.64 points (0.53%) to 18,169.07, while the FBM Shariah gained 73.48 points (0.58%) to 12,660.09, reflecting sustained investor appetite for higher-beta and domestically driven stocks outside the heavyweight index.

Trading activity remained active, with turnover concentrated in retail and technology-linked names.

Luster Industries Bhd led the most active list with over 217.4 million shares traded, followed by Inari Amertron Bhd, which surged 24 sen to RM2.06, and Zetrix AI Bhd, which added one sen to 87 sen.

VS Industry Bhd held steady at 22.5 sen, while AirAsia X Bhd slipped one sen to RM1.24.

Market sentiment pointed to selective positioning, with investors favouring counters tied to domestic consumption, industrial activity and technology over traditional large-cap defensives.

The divergence between the benchmark and broader indices suggests confidence remains intact, but increasingly focused on segments perceived to offer stronger near-term earnings visibility.

data.title: Bursa Edges Higher At Midday, KLCI Up 0.18%
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia traded mildly higher at midday, with the FBM KLCI edging up 3.01 points or 0.18% to 1,720.28 as selective buying in key counters helped offset mixed broader sentiment.

The broader market indices also posted modest gains. The FBM70 rose 0.09% to 18,185.52, while the FBM EMAS and FBM Small Cap indices each added 0.13%.

Trading activity remained firm in selected small and mid-cap names, with UEM Sunrise Bhd (UEMS) among the most actively traded stocks, gaining 6 sen or 10% to 65 sen on heavy volume of 672.6 million shares.

MRCB also advanced 1.5 sen or 4.8% to 33 sen, while Zetrix AI Bhd slipped 1 sen to 86 sen despite strong turnover.

On the gainers’ board, Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd led advances, rising 54 sen to RM31.22, followed by KESM Industries Bhd, which surged 43 sen to RM4.16. United Malacca Bhd, Dutch Lady Milk Industries Bhd and United Plantations Bhd also posted gains.

Meanwhile, losses were led by MPI, which fell 82 sen to RM37.06, followed by Nestlé (Malaysia) Bhd down 82 sen to RM98.30 and Petronas Dagangan Bhd, which slipped 56 sen to RM19.76.

Market sentiment remained broadly cautious with rotational trading dominating, as investors continued to take positions in selected consumer, property and plantation counters while trimming exposure in defensive blue chips.

data.title: Gold Slides To Three-Week Low As US-Iran Talks And Central Bank Decisions Loom
data.description:
Gold prices slipped to a three-week low on Tuesday as investors weighed geopolitical tensions and upcoming central bank decisions, with inflation concerns kept alive by firm crude oil prices.

Spot gold fell 1.1% to US$4,628.88 per ounce as of 0553 GMT, its lowest since April 7. US gold futures for June delivery also dropped 1.1% to US$4,643.70.

The decline came as uncertainty persisted over US-Iran relations, with US President Donald Trump reported to be unhappy with the latest Iranian proposal aimed at resolving the ongoing two-month conflict. The war has disrupted energy supplies, fuelled inflation pressures and led to thousands of deaths.

“Geopolitical headlines are still the main driver (of gold prices). In the event of a deal (between the US and Iran) or an interim deal, the dollar should weaken, and gold will likely break out to the upside,” said Edward Meir, analyst at Marex.

Oil prices remained above US$110 per barrel as the Strait of Hormuz stayed largely disrupted, adding to inflationary pressures. Higher crude costs typically raise transport and production expenses, which can reduce expectations for interest rate cuts.

While gold is often viewed as an inflation hedge, higher interest rates tend to make yield-bearing assets more attractive, weighing on the metal’s appeal.

Investors are now awaiting the US Federal Reserve’s two-day policy meeting, with rates widely expected to remain unchanged on Wednesday. Attention is also turning to upcoming decisions from the European Central Bank, Bank of England and Bank of Canada.

“The Fed is going to be largely irrelevant. They’re not going to be doing anything on rates for now,” Meir added, noting potential cuts later in the year if global growth slows.

Elsewhere in precious metals, silver dropped 3% to US$73.23 per ounce, platinum fell 1.5% to US$1,953.50 and palladium declined 2.1% to US$1,445.50.

Reuters

data.title: Straits Times Index Falls 0.46% At Start Of Trade
data.description:
Singapore shares opened lower, tracking a cautious regional mood as oil prices surged and geopolitical tensions between the United States and Iran intensified over the weekend.

The Straits Times Index (STI) slipped 0.46% to 4,899.98 in early trade as markets reacted to higher energy prices and weaker sentiment in US futures.

Across the broader market, trading remained relatively balanced with 141 gainers and 138 decliners. Total volume stood at 328.79 million shares, valued at S$253.93 million, reflecting muted participation at the open.

The weakness came after Brent crude climbed more than 2% to a three-week high of US$107.97 per barrel, driven by renewed concerns over supply disruptions linked to stalled US Iran negotiations and continued uncertainty over the Strait of Hormuz.

Sentiment was further pressured after S&P 500 futures dipped 0.3% following record highs on Wall Street last week, suggesting investors were turning more cautious amid rising geopolitical risks. The US dollar also strengthened modestly against major currencies, reinforcing a broader risk-off tone.

US President Donald Trump said dialogue with Iran remained possible but under strict conditions, stating that any agreement would require Tehran to abandon nuclear ambitions. However, efforts to restart talks appeared to lose momentum after diplomatic engagements were disrupted over the weekend.

Elsewhere in Singapore’s market landscape, broader indices showed mixed performance with S-REIT and regional benchmark indices holding steady, while derivatives activity remained subdued in early trading.

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Against Dollar, Lower Against Majors
data.description:
The ringgit edged higher against the US dollar but was traded mostly lower against other major currencies in early trade on Monday amid subdued market sentiment ahead of the US Federal Reserve meeting later this week, Bernama reported. 

At 8.14 am, the local currency was marginally higher at 3.9610/9680 against the greenback, compared with 3.9630/9670 at Friday’s close.

SPI Asset Management managing partner Stephen Innes said the foreign exchange market is currently subdued, a typical pattern ahead of a US Federal Reserve meeting as traders assess whether the policy guidance will lean hawkish or dovish.

The Federal Open Market Committee meeting will be held on April 28 and 29. 

Innes said positioning has narrowed and conviction has eased, with market participants largely holding back until there is greater clarity.

On the geopolitical side, Innes said the lack of progress in the Iran peace talks is acting as a mild drag on risk sentiment and likely capped what could have been a broader dollar sell-off earlier in the week.

“That said, unless we see a meaningful military escalation that drives oil materially higher, the impact should stay contained. For now, I’d expect a modest bias toward ringgit appreciation, but nothing aggressive.

“The broader setup still leans toward range trading, as the dollar is unlikely to weaken meaningfully with oil holding around current levels. That should keep the downside in US dollar contained, limiting any sustained ringgit rally,” he told Bernama.

At the opening, the ringgit depreciated against a basket of major currencies.

It fell against the British pound to 5.3529/3624 from 5.3429/3483 at Friday’s close, declined against the Japanese yen to 2.4831/4876 from Friday’s 2.4808/4834 and eased against the euro to 4.6379/6461 from Friday’s 4.6312/6358.  

The local currency traded mixed against ASEAN peers.

It eased against the Singapore dollar to 3.1020/1078 from 3.1009/1043 at Friday’s close, and was slightly higher against the Thai baht to 12.2181/2465 from 12.2183/2363. 

It edged up against the Indonesian rupiah at 229.9/230.4 from 230.0/230.3 and was almost flat versus the Philippine peso at 6.52/6.54 from 6.52/6.53.

data.title: Dollar Holds Steady As Markets Weigh Shaky US-Iran Diplomacy
data.description:
The dollar held steady on Monday as investors assessed uneven progress in United States-Iran negotiations aimed at ending a two-month conflict, with sentiment clouded by uncertainty over whether a lasting ceasefire can hold and the strategic Strait of Hormuz remaining effectively closed.

Geopolitical tensions stayed in focus after United States President Donald Trump cancelled a planned visit to Islamabad by his envoys, saying Iran could reach out if it wanted to negotiate an end to the war. The move followed reports that diplomatic efforts had stalled even as the ceasefire paused full-scale fighting that began with strikes in late February.

Sentiment briefly improved after Axios reported, citing sources, that Iran had sent a new proposal via Pakistani mediators covering reopening of the waterway and an end to hostilities, with nuclear talks pushed to a later stage. Markets, however, remained cautious as no formal agreement has been reached.

Currency markets reflected the wait-and-see tone. The euro was steady at $1.1724 while sterling traded at $1.3536. The dollar index stood at 98.491, holding recent gains after earlier safe-haven demand faded as hopes of a near-term resolution emerged.

Analysts noted markets may be underpricing risks. One senior financial analyst said confidence in progress appeared “perhaps even blase” and warned that “if it doesn’t the markets will have to re-price quite violently”.

Attention is also turning to central banks this week, with the Bank of Japan expected to keep rates unchanged but signal possible tightening later in the year. The yen remained weak at 159.51 per dollar, hovering near levels that could prompt intervention.

The Federal Reserve, European Central Bank and Bank of England are all expected to hold rates steady while assessing the inflation impact from higher energy prices.

Reuters

data.title: Weakening Macroeconomics Outlook Weighs On Premium Growth
data.description:
MBSB Investment Bank Bhd (MBSB Research) maintained its POSITIVE call on the insurance sector, with Buy calls on Allianz and Syarikat Takaful Malaysia and target prices of RM23.74 and RM3.95 respectively, citing resilient earnings momentum despite emerging headwinds from the prolonged Middle East conflict.

The research house said the sector should deliver a solid first quarter for calendar year 2026, extending the strong momentum seen in late 2025, as the full impact of the war has yet to filter through. Growth across both general and life segments is expected to remain firm, supported by controlled claims and a relatively strong ringgit which has kept import-related costs manageable.

However, the analysts warned that conditions may turn more challenging in subsequent quarters as logistical disruptions linked to the conflict push up global drug and medical equipment prices.

They noted that medical claims inflation, which eased to 5.3% in 2025, could rise again in 2026, with local authorities already flagging increases of up to 30% to 40% for certain medicines and devices.

MBSB Research added that a weakening macroeconomic outlook could further weigh on premium growth, particularly in the mass market, as rising living costs begin to affect consumer spending and loan demand. General insurance demand may soften alongside slower vehicle and property purchases, while bancatakaful-driven growth could moderate if financing activity weakens.

Despite these risks, the analysts said valuations remain attractive relative to regional peers and dividend yields continue to sit at mid-single-digit levels, supporting the sector’s appeal.

They also highlighted that exposure to marine and aviation takaful remains limited, suggesting minimal direct impact from disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz.

data.title: Korea Passes UK To Become World’s Eighth-Largest Stock Market
data.description:
South Korea has leapfrogged the UK to become the world’s eighth-biggest stock market, fueled by a high-octane rally in its artificial intelligence-linked technology champions.

Bloomberg reported, the total market capitalization of Korean-listed companies has surged more than 45% this year to $4.04 trillion, while the UK’s has climbed about 3% to $3.99 trillion, according to data compiled by Bloomberg. The size of the UK market was about double that of Korea’s as recently as the end of 2024.

The surge in Korean stocks underscores the global pivot toward companies linked to artificial intelligence, which has driven gains in the Asian nation’s two biggest listed companies: Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc.. The two memory-chip behemoths now account for more than 40% of the total market cap of the benchmark Kospi index, which has more than 800 constituents. A further tailwind has been provided by President Lee Jae Myung’s push to bolster equity prices via corporate governance reforms and pro-market policies.

“The rapid ascent of Korea and Taiwan reflects a structural rebalancing in global equity markets, driven by their dominance in AI hardware rather than tactical asset allocation,” said Francesco Chan, emerging markets and Asia Pacific investment specialist at JPMorgan Asset Management in Hong Kong. “As the backbone of the AI supply chain — with a ‘super-cycle’ advantage in high-end foundries and memory — these economies are attracting sustained structural capital inflows.”

The rally in Korean stocks mirrors that of Taiwan’s, which in April also overtook the UK to become the world’s seventh-largest share market. Taiwan’s gains have also been powered by the world’s largest chip foundry, Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co., which now accounts for about 45% of the island’s benchmark gauge. Taiwan’s stock market value, now at $4.48 trillion, is now approaching that of Canada.

The UK’s FTSE 100 Index has gained about 4% this year, not much less than the MSCI all country world index, but well behind the supercharged gains of markets that have benefited from the AI boom. The nation’s equity market, which is Europe’s biggest, remains dominated by traditional sectors such as financials, consumer staples and energy-and-mining firms.

Forces such as the potential of AI, global defense spending and corporate government reforms “support a much steeper trajectory for Korean and Taiwanese equities than for Europe,” said Patrick Kellenberger, an emerging-market equity strategist at Lombard Odier in Geneva. “Europe continues to struggle with commercializing and scaling innovation. Creating the conditions for innovative firms to emerge and grow is critical — but also time consuming.”

While equity values have soared for the Asian chip powerhouses, economies in the region remain much smaller than those of the major European ones. South Korea’s gross domestic product is estimated to be $1.9 trillion this year and Taiwan’s at $977 billion, well below the forecasts for more than $3 trillion for Germany, the UK and France, based on estimates from the International Monetary Fund.

For now, Wall Street strategists remain bullish over Korean stocks, citing earnings upgrades from AI demand and favorable valuations. Goldman Sachs Group Inc. has raised its Kospi target to 8,000, mainly driven by an increase of more than 200% in its 2026 earnings growth projection.

data.title: Bitcoin Nears US$80,000, Ethereum Extends Rally On Institutional Demand
data.description:
Bitcoin and Ethereum traded higher on April 27, with renewed institutional inflows and improving macro sentiment pushing the two largest cryptocurrencies toward key resistance levels.

Bitcoin hovered around US$78,600 to US$79,100 during the session on April 27, marking gains of roughly 1% to 2% over the previous day and placing the token within striking distance of the psychologically significant US$80,000 mark.

Ethereum also strengthened, trading near US$2,370 to US$2,390, extending its month-long recovery as investors rotated back into risk assets. The world’s second-largest cryptocurrency has gained more than 8% over the past month, supported by improving sentiment across digital assets.

Market watchers said Bitcoin’s rally is being underpinned by strong institutional accumulation, including sustained inflows into spot Bitcoin ETFs and fresh corporate treasury purchases.

Analysts cited by market reports noted that institutional demand has helped offset lingering macro concerns, with Bitcoin climbing above US$79,000 for the first time in weeks. However, the US$78,000-US$80,000 range is emerging as a crucial resistance zone where traders may begin taking profits.

Ethereum’s advance has been more measured but remains notable as the asset continues to outperform broader expectations after months of subdued trading. Recent forecasts suggest Ethereum’s resilience above the US$2,300 support band could pave the way for further upside if momentum holds.

The broader crypto market capitalisation stood near US$2.7 trillion on April 27, reflecting a steady rebound in investor confidence after heightened geopolitical uncertainty earlier in the month.

For now, traders are watching whether Bitcoin can decisively break through US$80,000, a move that could trigger the next leg of the rally, while Ethereum’s ability to hold above US$2,350 may determine whether altcoins continue to track higher in the near term.

data.title: HSIF Stays Under Pressure As Bearish Momentum Holds Below Key Resistance
data.description:
The Hang Seng Index Futures (HSIF) remained in a bearish consolidation phase as it continued trading below a key resistance level, with technical indicators pointing to sustained downside pressure, according to RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research).

The futures contract closed lower by 35 points at 25,930 points, after moving between an intraday high of 26,095 points and a low of 25,826 points. In the evening session, it weakened further, last traded at 25,875 points.

RHB Research noted that HSIF remains below the 26,600-point resistance, indicating that bearish control is still intact. It added that the 50-day simple moving average is turning lower, reinforcing negative momentum in the near term.

“The latest session saw the index trading below the 26,600-point resistance level, showing that the bears still have the technical advantage,” the research house said.

The firm maintained a negative trading bias and advised keeping the existing short position initiated on Feb 26 at 26,367 points. A stop-loss level remains set at 26,600 points.

If downward pressure continues, HSIF is expected to drift towards the 25,100-point support level, followed by 24,500 points. On the upside, immediate resistance is seen at 26,600 points with a stronger barrier at 27,200 points.

RHB Research added that as long as HSIF stays below key resistance levels, the broader technical outlook is likely to remain bearish.

data.title: Banking, Plantation Decliners Weigh On Bursa As Losers Edge Ahead
data.description:
Selling pressure in banking and plantation counters capped Bursa Malaysia’s session on April 27, with decliners narrowly outpacing gainers despite strong buying interest in selected technology and telecom stocks.

At the close, the market recorded 599 losers against 579 gainers, while 617 counters ended unchanged, reflecting a mixed but cautious trading environment.

Among the biggest drags were Hong Leong Bank Bhd, which fell 44 sen to RM22.34, and Hong Leong Financial Group Bhd, down 30 sen to RM19.

Plantation heavyweight United Plantations Bhd also retreated 70 sen to RM31.62, while consumer names such as Heineken Malaysia Bhd and Nestlé (M) Bhd slipped 18 sen each.

Despite the broader weakness, selective buying in blue chips and semiconductor-linked stocks provided pockets of strength.

Telekom Malaysia Bhd led the gainers, rising 48 sen to RM7.58, followed by Fraser & Neave Holdings Bhd, up 38 sen to RM30.68.

Industrial and tech counters also attracted attention, with Press Metal Aluminium Holdings Bhd climbing 38 sen to RM8.80, while Inari Amertron Bhd surged 24 sen to RM2.06 on heavy volume of close to 113 million shares. Unisem (M) Bhd added 22 sen to RM3.30.

Trading activity remained robust, with turnover reaching 3.742 billion shares valued at RM3.304 billion.

The session underscored a rotational market, where gains in technology and telecommunications were offset by profit-taking in financials, plantations and consumer staples.

data.title: Green Start For Bursa As Key Indices Post Early Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened firmer, tracking steadier global sentiment as US equities posted modest gains overnight amid cautious trading ahead of a packed week of corporate earnings, central bank meetings and geopolitical developments.

The FBM KLCI rose 5.08 points or 0.30% to 1,722.35 at 9.34 am, trading between 1,720.73 and 1,727.72 in early session moves.

Broader market indices also edged higher at the open. The FBM70 gained 9.77 points or 0.05% to 18,178.84 while the FBMEMAS added 30.51 points or 0.24% to 12,746.64. The FBMSHA advanced 21.12 points or 0.17% to 12,681.21 and the F4GBM rose 2.32 points or 0.23% to 1,029.69.

Market breadth was positive with advancers leading decliners 136 to 70, while total market volume stood at 187.37 million shares worth S$188.34 million.

Among the most active stocks, Luster rose 0.005 to 0.045 while UEM Sunrise added 0.035 to 0.625. WCT gained 0.030 to 0.455, while Bintai slipped 0.005 to 0.085.

Top gainers included UMS Integration up 0.210 to 6.750 and VSTECS rising 0.180 to 5.240. KESM added 0.170 to 3.900 while United Plantations gained 0.160 to 31.780. Fraser & Neave climbed 0.120 to 30.800.

On the losers’ list, MPI fell 0.620 to 37.260 while Allianz dropped 0.260 to 20.900. Petronas Dagangan eased 0.220 to 20.100 while Nestlé slipped 0.140 to 98.980 and Riverview declined 0.140 to 2.850.

Sentiment across regional markets was supported by a mild risk-on tone after US equities closed higher overnight. The S&P 500 rose 0.12% while the Nasdaq Composite added 0.20% as investors balanced optimism over earnings with caution ahead of key macroeconomic events including Federal Reserve policy signals and ongoing geopolitical tensions.

Oil prices also extended gains on continued supply concerns linked to the US-Iran conflict, with disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz keeping energy markets on edge and supporting broader inflation watch themes globally.

data.title: Wall Street Pauses As Earnings Storm Looms
data.description:
US equities closed with modest gains as investors turned cautious at the start of a packed week dominated by earnings, central bank decisions and geopolitical developments.

The S&P 500 rose 0.12% to 7,173.93 while the Nasdaq Composite gained 0.20% to 24,887.10. The Dow Jones Industrial Average slipped 0.13% to 49,168.04. Trading remained subdued as markets struggled to build on last week’s rally, with both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq hovering near record closing levels.

Investors are now focused on a wave of corporate earnings, with several major technology names due to report including Amazon, Alphabet, Meta Platforms, Apple and Microsoft. These companies are expected to offer clearer signals on returns from heavy investments in artificial intelligence.

“The market is just trying to deal with the rally that’s been going on and digest the latest all-time highs that we’ve made on the indices,” said Robert Pavlik, senior portfolio manager at Dakota Wealth. “And it’s trying to figure out whether or not those all-time highs are justified.”

Earnings season has so far delivered a strong showing, with 81% of S&P 500 companies beating expectations. Analysts now project earnings growth of 16.1% year on year, up from earlier estimates.

Geopolitical tensions remain a key overhang. Efforts to revive talks between the US and Iran continue after President Donald Trump cancelled a planned envoy visit, while disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz persist.

“Guidance has been pretty good. We’re seeing earnings growth of 15%, and I would classify that as a very good environment, except the road has gotten a lot more bumpy,” Pavlik added.

Market attention is also turning to the Federal Reserve, which begins its policy meeting this week. Rates are widely expected to remain unchanged, though investors will look for signals on inflation and economic conditions.

Among individual stocks, Verizon gained after raising its outlook while Domino’s Pizza fell following weaker sales. Nvidia extended gains and reclaimed a market value above US$5 trillion, highlighting continued strength in artificial intelligence-driven stocks.

Reuters

data.title: Ringgit Opens Higher Against Asians, Flat Towards Dollar
data.description:
The ringgit traded mostly higher against other major and Asian currencies in early trade today amid cautious sentiment in the currency market.

The domestic unit, however, was little changed against the US dollar as investors adopted a wait-and-see stance ahead of the Federal Open Market Committee’s (FOMC) third meeting of the year, an analyst said, according to Bernama.

At 8.18 am, the local currency stood at 3.9505/9550 against the greenback, compared with 3.9505/9545 at Monday’s close.

Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd chief economist Mohd Afzanizam Abdul Rashid said the ringgit was expected to trade within a narrow range today amid cautious market sentiment.

“Traders and investors will be observing the latest assessment by the FOMC members when they conclude their two-day meeting on April 29,” he told Bernama. 

At the opening, the ringgit appreciated against a basket of major currencies.

It rose against the British pound to 5.3482/3543 from 5.3525/3580 at Monday’s close, was higher against the Japanese yen to 2.4791/4821 from Monday’s  2.4810/4837, and eased against the euro to 4.6316/6368 from Monday’s 4.6387/6434.

The local currency traded mostly higher against ASEAN peers.

It went up against the Singapore dollar to 3.1011/1051 from 3.1018/1052 at Monday’s close, and rose against the Thai baht to 12.2061/2257 from yesterday’s close of 12.2129/2309. 

The domestic unit was flat against the Indonesian rupiah and the Philippine peso at 229.5/229.8 and 6.50/6.51, respectively.

data.title: Foreign Exchange Rates April 27, 2026
data.description:
The closing foreign exchange rates, sourced from Bank Negara Malaysia and recorded at 5pm on April 27, 2026, provide a comprehensive overview of currency fluctuations for the day.

These rates, crucial for investors and businesses alike, reflect the relative strength or weakness of major global currencies against the Malaysian Ringgit. They serve as a vital indicator for assessing international trade competitiveness, investment opportunities and overall economic trends in the global market.

The exchange rates are as shown below:

data.title: Gold Holds Near Record Levels As Traders Brace For Central Bank Signals
data.description:
Gold prices stayed near historic highs on April 27 as investors adopted a cautious stance ahead of upcoming central bank meetings, with stubbornly elevated oil prices fuelling concerns over a more hawkish monetary policy outlook.

Reuters reported that spot gold edged down 0.1% to US$4,704.01 per oz, while US gold futures for June delivery slipped 0.4% to US$4,719.90.

The modest retreat follows a strong rally that has kept bullion close to record territory, supported by safe-haven demand and persistent geopolitical and inflation concerns.

Analysts said traders are now recalibrating expectations as central banks prepare to weigh inflation risks against slowing global growth.

A key concern is the resilience in oil prices, which could complicate efforts to ease interest rates by keeping inflationary pressures elevated.

Higher-for-longer rates typically reduce the appeal of non-yielding assets such as gold, prompting some investors to lock in profits after recent gains.

Still, bullion’s ability to remain above the US$4,700-mark underscores continued demand for defensive assets in an uncertain macro environment.

Market participants are watching whether central bank commentary this week reinforces expectations of tighter policy, a factor that could determine whether gold extends its rally or consolidates at current levels.

For now, the precious metal remains one of the strongest-performing assets of the year, with traders balancing safe-haven flows against shifting rate expectations.

data.title: Stock Picks: Sunway REIT And Edelteq
data.description:
RHB Investment Bank Bhd (RHB Research) is turning bullish on Sunway REIT and Edelteq Holdings Berhad, pointing to technical breakouts that signal further upside potential in the near term.

The research house said Sunway REIT has entered a bullish setup after breaking above the RM2.43 resistance level, supported by a strong candlestick formation and sustained positioning above its 21-day simple moving average. Riding on this momentum, the counter could climb towards RM2.55, with a further upside target at RM2.65 if buying interest holds. However, a drop below the RM2.30 support level may invalidate the bullish trend and trigger a correction.

Meanwhile, Edelteq is also showing strong upward momentum after breaking past the RM0.39 resistance on rising trading volume. RHB Research noted that the formation of a long bullish candlestick signals continued buying strength, with the stock potentially advancing towards RM0.42 and then RM0.45. On the downside, a fall below RM0.36 could signal the start of a corrective phase.

RHB Research said both counters are exhibiting positive technical signals, with price action and volume trends indicating sustained investor interest in the near term.

data.title: Stock Today: Cropmate Slips 2.5% As Accounts Frozen Under AML Investigation
data.description:
Shares in Cropmate Bhd came under selling pressure today after the fertiliser manufacturer disclosed that several of its bank accounts and those of its subsidiaries were temporarily frozen under an order issued by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) yesterday.

As at 11.55 am, Cropmate was last done at 19.5 sen, down 0.5 sen or 2.5% from the previous close. The counter traded between 19 sen and 20 sen, with 25.54 million shares changing hands.

Buying interest stood at 19.5 sen with 7.05 million shares queued, while selling pressure was heavier at 20 sen with 29.69 million shares on offer.

The stock weakness followed the company’s announcement that the account freeze was carried out under Section 44(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLATFA) as part of an ongoing investigation.

Cropmate said it was notified on April 24 of the order and described the action as a standard procedure during investigations. The company stressed that it has no indication the matter involves misconduct by its management or employees.

It also said it is cooperating fully with authorities and is working to secure the release of the affected accounts under Section 44A of AMLATFA.

Despite the development, Cropmate maintained that its operations remain unaffected, adding that it continues to have access to alternative banking facilities to support business activities.

The company said it does not expect any material financial or operational impact, with business continuing as usual and customers and partners unaffected.

data.title: Bursa Opens Higher As Banking And Tech Stocks Lead Gains
data.description:
Bursa Malaysia opened higher, with broad-based gains across major indices as investors tracked global markets unsettled by geopolitical tensions and rising oil prices.

The FBM KLCI rose 10.62 points or 0.62% to 1,730.96 in early trade, reflecting stronger sentiment across heavyweight counters.

The FBM70 index advanced 138.47 points or 0.77% to 18,210.90 while the FBM Emas climbed 80.38 points or 0.63% to 12,796.10. The FBM Emas Shariah gained 100.29 points or 0.80% to 12,686.90, and the F4GBM Index increased 6.54 points or 0.64% to 1,033.67.

Market breadth was positive with most key indices trading higher at the open, supported by selective buying in index-linked stocks and mid-cap counters.

Sentiment was shaped by a cautious global backdrop after Brent crude surged more than 2% to a three-week high of US$107.97 per barrel, driven by ongoing supply concerns linked to stalled US Iran negotiations and disruptions around key Gulf shipping routes.

US stock futures also slipped 0.3%, suggesting some profit-taking after Wall Street’s recent record highs. The US dollar strengthened slightly against major currencies, reflecting a mild risk-off tone across global markets.

Among active counters, Luster led trading volumes, followed by VS, JSB, AMS and VELESTO, while top gainers included Nestle, MPI, PMETAL, TM and Vitrox. Decliners were more limited, led by Chin Teck, Padini, BAT, Kossan and SOP.
